summary: High‑energy 1978 show with a smoking Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World sequence and solid soundboard recording
review: The performance is praised for its tight first set, a standout second set featuring an incredible Estimated Prophet > Eyes of the World stretch, killer Bertha, and a memorable Werewolves of London encore. The soundboard source provides clear, well‑balanced audio that most reviewers describe as “nice” or “great” with only minor subjective critiques.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high star ratings (mostly 4‑5) and enthusiastic comments about both the music and the soundboard quality lead to a strong combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple reviewers note “nice audio quality”, “great sound”, “tight mix” and overall clarity; no major drop‑outs or balance problems mentioned.

standout_songs: Estimated Prophet > Eyes of the World
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set praised for tight song selection; second set flows smoothly with Bertha → Good Lovin' → Ship of Fools and a high‑energy encore, keeping the momentum alive.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly highlighted – tearing it up with wah, jaw‑dropping middle solo on Estimated Prophet, smiling/dancing onstage, and a unique lilting motif in Bertha.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for an impressive bass run on The Other One (N.F.A.) and generally “sweet” vocal presence.
band_member_comments.Bob: Mentioned for solid slide work and strong stage presence, especially in the interplay with Jerry.
